summary: Typosquat of @playwright/test forcing install of sibling typosquat dependency
details: Package is published as `venturo-playwright-runner` but falsifies its identity to impersonate Microsoft Playwright: `package.json` sets `author.name` to `Microsoft Corporation`, `repository.url` to `github.com/microsoft/playwright`, and homepage to `playwright.dev`. The vendored sources appear copied from `@playwright/test`, but `dependencies` declares `venturo-playwright-core@1.0.3` — a sibling typosquat of the real `playwright-core` — which is automatically installed on any `npm install` of this package, pulling unaudited code into the installer's dependency tree. A further inconsistency confirms a hastily-renamed copy: `index.js` line 17 does `module.exports = require('playwright-core')`, but `playwright-core` is not declared in `dependencies` (only the typosquat `venturo-playwright-core` is). The wrapper is the delivery vehicle; the actual payload risk lives in the sibling typosquat dep that this package forces onto installers.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
